closure_rule: All target gate records exist and target zero-grey gate passes
TLS is implemented on the route and plaintext or invalid proof is refused
Catalog object descriptors include required UUID lifecycle epoch policy transaction audit and synonym descriptor fields
Comments and descriptions are resolver rows and not duplicated as object authority
Required sys.catalog tables including synonym descriptors are materialized with UUID authority and physical index profiles
Readable catalog projections use resolver names and security filtering
sys.information views exist with resolver-backed names security filtering and sys.information_schema is a first-class synonym object resolving to the same final schema UUID
DDL generation publication commits or rolls back under MGA
SqlObjectReference binding is ordered profile-aware security-checked synonym-aware and UUID-only after final target binding
Dependency graph stores traverses and validates synonym target UUID edges with DDL validation
Driver metadata is derived from descriptor UUID authority and does not leak forbidden UUIDs
Catalog invalid states emit stable diagnostics
Constraints and keys enforce through catalog authority and MGA transaction visibility
MGA archive backup delta and PITR are derived evidence streams and never recovery authority outside MGA
Backup and restore manifests include coverage checksum signature key-lineage and timeline proof
Checkpoint recovery and cleanup integrate with transaction inventory and page state
Delta package and apply validate contiguous non-authoritative coverage
PITR target selection validates coverage and replays deterministically
Dirty manifest is emitted hashed persisted and consumed by recovery
MGA visibility publication follows durability fences and allocation maps never point at non-durable pages
